---
title: "Eurojackpot Statistical Analysis"
---

## 🎮 What is Eurojackpot?

Eurojackpot is one of Europe's most popular transnational lottery games, launched in 2012 and now played across 18 European countries. The game features a simple yet challenging format: players must match **5 numbers from a pool of 50** plus **2 Euro numbers from a pool of 12** to win the jackpot. Draws take place every Tuesday and Friday in Helsinki, with jackpots starting at €10 million and capable of rolling over to a maximum of €120 million.

## 📊 About This Analysis

This project examines historical Eurojackpot draw data through statistical analysis. We'll explore:

- **Historical Draw Data** - Comprehensive examination of all Eurojackpot draws since inception
- **Number Frequencies** - Which numbers have appeared most and least frequently over time
- **Distribution Patterns** - How the drawn numbers distribute across the possible range
- **Statistical Observations** - Interesting statistical properties of the historical results

## 🔍 Analytical Approach

Our analysis employs descriptive statistics and data visualization techniques to examine the Eurojackpot draw history. We present factual observations about past draws while acknowledging the fundamental principle that each draw is an independent random event.

All visualizations and calculations are implemented using R and its data visualization packages, providing both statistical detail and visual clarity.

<div class="info-box">
<strong>Important Note:</strong> This analysis examines historical data only. Lottery draws are designed to be random events, and past results cannot predict future outcomes. This content is provided purely for informational and educational purposes.
</div>

### Content Overview

- **Data Exploration**: Examination of the dataset structure and basic statistics
- **Number Analysis**: Detailed look at the frequency distribution of drawn numbers
- **Temporal Observations**: How draw patterns have appeared over different time periods
- **Statistical Insights**: Notable statistical properties within the historical data

<div class="navigation-buttons">
  <a href="data-overview.html" class="next-button">Next: Data Overview <i class="fa fa-arrow-right"></i></a>
</div>
